Journal.Title: Journal of the American College of Cardiology

Article.ArticleTitle: Simultaneous or Sequential Initiation of ARNI and SGLT2I in Patients With HFrEF: The INITIATE-HFrEF Trial.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: RISE-Health, Department of Surgery and Physiology, Faculty of Medicine, University of Porto, Porto, Portugal; University of Lorraine, National Institute of Health and Medical Research (INSERM), Clinical Research Center 1433, Nancy Regional University Hospital, INSERM 1116 and Cardiovascular and Renal Clinical Trialists (INI-CRCT), French Clinical Research Infrastructure Network (F-CRIN), Nancy, France; Internal Medicine Service, Gaia/Espinho Local Health Unit, Gaia, Portugal. KeywordList.Keyword: heart failure with reduced ejection fraction

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: Funding Support and Author Disclosures This study was funded by Novartis; the study also received national funds through the Foundation for Science and Technology (Fundação para a Ciência e Tecnologia [FCT]), under the scope of the Cardiovascular Research and Development Center (UnIC; UIDB/00051/2020 and UIDP/00051/2020). Dr Oliveira has received PhD grant support from the FCT. Dr Pereira-Sousa has received PhD grant support from the FCT. Dr Saraiva has received national funds through the FCT and from the Recovery and Resilience Plan (PRR), in the scope of Science Plus Training (Ciência Mais Capacitação), under the FCT Tenure Program (2023.13694.TENURE.021). All other authors have reported that they have no relationships relevant to the contents of this paper to disclose.
